授業目的: 世界にはなぜ豊かな国と貧しい国があるのか？なぜアジア諸国がラテンアメリカ諸国よりも早く発展してきたのか？国際貿易、移民労働者、対外投資が国の経済成長率と国内所得分配にどのような影響を与えているのか？この科目では、様々な視点からこれらの課題に関する知識と理解を深めることを目指しています。
到達目標: 1. データを使って経済問題と社会問題を客観的に分析できるようになる2. 開発経済学の理論に基づいて、過去と現在の経済発展の背景を説明できるようになる3. グローバルな視点を身に着け、経済開発について批判的に考える力を身に着ける
授業方法: This is a lecture-style class that will follow the contents of the textbook "Economic Development" by Michael P. Todaro, Stephen C. Smith (see 'Reference books'). Depending on the number of students, some classes might feature group work or group discussions.

第2回:
【Core Weeks①】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Measuring development: income and wealth
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Measuring inequality: the Gini index
第4回:
【Core Weeks③】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
The UN Human Development Index and Multidimensional Poverty Index
第5回:
【Core Weeks④】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Classic theories of economic growth
第6回:
【Core Weeks⑤】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Exogenous theories of economic growth
第7回:
【Core Weeks⑥】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Endogenous theories of economic growth
第8回:
【Core Weeks⑦】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
The labor market, demographics and migration
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑧】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Human capital and the role of education
第10回:
【Core Weeks⑨】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Minorities, religion, values, conflict and crime
第11回:
【Core Weeks⑩】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Colonial legacy, political systems, institutions
第12回:
【Core Weeks⑪】
"Face-to-face class"/ 《90minutes》
Health and development: the roles of healthcare and nutrition
第14回:
【Flex Hours】 
"Online class（Task-oriented format）"/ 《90minutes》
Foreign direct investment and development

参考書/Reference books1: Michael P. Todaro, Stephen C. Smith
Economic Development (The Pearson Series in Economics)
参考書/Reference books2: 開発経済学入門 第2版
参考書/Reference books3: マイケル・P. トダロ, ステファン・C. スミス
トダロとスミスの開発経済学
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: It is not necessary to purchase any books.
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
